name: Ben Jealous
subject: Politicians with strings
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend,
I'm in this race, running for people determined to solve the big problems affecting all of our families.
I am not a career politician, but the outpouring of support for this campaign shows that Marylanders are hungry for _a different kind of leadership._
**They want leadership that 's beholden to no one except the people they're sworn to serve.**
That's why I refuse to take money from big business because the economy should be for everyone.
When a business writes a candidate a check, it's a fundamentally transactional relationship: they give you money because they want something in return. And that something might not be good for the people who elect you.
We don't need politicians coming into office with strings that tie them to preserving the status quo.
Our campaign doesn't just have more Maryland donors than any other candidate. As of the January filing deadline, 99% of our donations were from individual donors just like you.
**[Will you chip $3 in today help us continue building our movement?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7c7/7ab0e2c4/7fa4aeba/349a5dcc/2306783150/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDQxOThDIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st4-18?refcode=OHQ04198C")**
We have more low dollar contributions from regular families than all the other Maryland campaigns combined, including the governor's. And we don't take a dollar from special interests.
This is a different kind of campaign. We can keep on pushing, and with your help, friend, we can win.
Onward,
Ben

**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7c7/7ab0e2c4/7fa4aeba/349a5dcc/2306783150/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDQxOThDIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st4-18?refcode=OHQ04198C")**

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER

Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7c7/7ab0e2c4/7fa4aeba/349a5dce/2306783150/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. If you'd like to [unsubscribe](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7c7/7ab0e2c4/7fa4aeba/349a5dc9/2306783150/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"), you can do so here. [Make a contribution to Ben and Susie here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a7c7/7ab0e2c4/7fa4aeba/349a5dc8/2306783150/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst_contribute?refcode=emfooter"). Contributions or gifts to Friends of Ben Jealous are not tax deductible.

name: The White House
subject: Meet the team that’s dismantling the illicit drug trade
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a5688cbd5652f5982c86cc9e83106be9f83f3ebfc2b2074cfd6b4e6901e7154fa1e460548bf33fab5efd77eda34ce2de1e) The White House • April 20, 2018
## Driving the Day
| • | Vice President Mike Pence will join Transportation Secretary Elaine Chao for a roundtable on infrastructure this afternoon. [ See the President’s plan.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a549574e7e100856ab8e5315baf313ed612b89b85574698e0b25dd099a9efaee17f1ebffb3bbcd57643ac87ea15feb32f0)
---|---
• | First Lady Melania Trump and President Donald J. Trump prepare to host French President Emmanuel Macron Tuesday for the first State Visit of the Trump Administration.
• | Today, Ivanka Trump and U.S. Treasurer Jovita Carranza travel to Rocky River, Ohio, for a town hall about wage hikes and boosted savings from President Trump’s tax cuts.

---
|

## Meet the team that’s dismantling the illicit drug trade
**President Trump visited the Joint Interagency Task Force South yesterday in Key West**. The Task Force is a crucial team that many Americans have likely never heard about. Led by top officials from the Armed Services and key Federal agencies, its efforts to dismantle the illicit drug trade have saved countless American lives.
**The Task Force faced record drug production during Fiscal Year 2017**. In response, it supported the removal of a record 283 metric tons of cocaine and 5 kilograms of heroin, as well as the detention of 900 suspected members of drug trafficking organizations. Helping to lead an international coalition, the Task Force oversees a staggering area of 42 million square miles across the globe.
**“We are behind you all the way,” President Trump said**. “And we want to thank you.” The President called on Congressional Democrats to support the Task Force by closing loopholes that allow drugs and human traffickers to spill into the United States. “We need border protection; we need the wall,” he said.
[President Trump visits the task force that saves American lives by fighting the flow of drugs.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a5fb29c53c9535a544d3888200e48a347c6aef0de98806f57f89d4a3d5ca7c620d3dfe0aa431e864bd0b590aa0d745a77f)

---
|

## ‘Right from the beginning we hit it off’
**The President concluded a productive summit with Japanese leader Shinzo Abe this week**. It marked the sixth meeting and third major summit between the two men since President Trump took office. “Right from the beginning we hit it off,” the President said. “The relationship is a very good one.”
**Denuclearization of North Korea topped the agenda**. With a meeting between President Trump and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un in the works, both the White House and Prime Minister Abe underscored that a global maximum pressure campaign on Pyongyang will continue until North Korea abandons all ballistic missiles and weapons of mass destruction. That point is nonnegotiable.
**American and Japan also agreed to intensify trade talks**. U.S. Trade Representative Robert Lighthizer will lead discussions for the U.S. side, guided by the President’s goal of free, fair, and reciprocal trade with each of our allies. “The relationship has been so good with all of us, and let’s see how we do with, right now, the trade deficit,” President Trump told Prime Minister Abe.
[Watch the short highlight reel](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a5374887b8fd9427c8c79d62127eaff94b0f43670a55f8f8d5f5312a30c1c1f9d7bb2657548e800dc5366f5b27650c2d80) or [read the full recap](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a52ae23a7431ff0f13dbb04a1e1b8fa692c7c44fa7e243e08bc63b8c41b4d0bedc98ebc9e7947d29d817e7fcc088b38577) of this week’s summit at Mar-a-Lago.

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
**President Donald J Trump, White House Chief of Staff John Kelly, and Secretary of Homeland Security Kirstjen Nielsen at the Joint Interagency Task Force South in Key West** | April 19, 2018

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a5a8357bd19e40b2336ec4dfede2242d6900f6ea2b3eff5e7c618bc9dc01021f6be9d5289e01f39a1f2c76f45b730c29e4) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0baca106f482a5a5aa659e840ff3dbccb260f046e553119d7b5adcc6a05ab1385895a15c518506906f1a11a43e916f89c222bb06420064c7) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4f5590130407a9c2b7b84f38db9a10387a3b80cfb6823f2dc4b61f9cc7eb76c21f680ee5028d8f3806ab831fbede1db2170ec489a5609d2bf)
---

subject: We have a plan to save the internet (signatures needed)
body:
Dear Activist,

**Big cable companies are about to have the power to distort the way the internet works. But we have a chance to stop them if enough of us take action right now.**

Here's the deal: on Monday, the FCC's decision to repeal net neutrality rules will take effect. This would give telecommunications companies the ability to potentially slow a website like Netflix down in an attempt to force you to use their preferred sites.

This kind of power could have grave consequence for our economy and our free speech rights, stifling innovative and activism fundamental to the internet as we know it.

**We have a plan to stop this from happening. All we need is one more Republican Senator to join us in supporting a resolution overturning the FCC 's decision.**

**Help us turn up the heat and get that vote:**

[**Tell the Republicans: Help us save net neutrality. Support our emergency petition right now pressuring the GOP to overturn the FCC 's attack on the open internet.**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xCybC/6/Ss/r5cs/61YLZJ/sPnwMPXCgW/P/P) ****[SIGN THE PETITION](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xCybC/s/Ss/r5cs/61YLZJ/7tNeqEGu/P/P)**** The basic principle of net neutrality is simple: anyone should be able to access any website or web service they want equally and anyone can make their own site or service and make it accessible to anyone with internet access.

It's a genius idea that enables entrepreneurship on a level playing field -- and that openness and sense of opportunity are particularly crucial for historically disadvantaged communities.

On the internet, anyone can become an overnight sensation based on the quality of their work, regardless of their gender, the color of their skin, who they love, or where they were born.

That's why we have to work hard to make our voices heard in this process -- especially since we're running out of time and we're so close to forcing a vote on overturning the FCC's awful decision to gut net neutrality.

**[Sign our petition pressuring the Senate Republicans to help us pass a resolution overturning the FCC's decision. We're so close here -- and your action today could make the difference](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xCybC/v/Ss/r5cs/61YLZJ/rlNtpUZMF/P/P).** ****[SIGN THE PETITION](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xCybC/z/Ss/r5cs/61YLZJ/Mm30XXUuI/P/P)**** Thanks for all you do,

--Team Kamala

clean_body: Dear Activist, Big cable companies are about to have the power to distort the way the internet works. But we have a chance to stop them if enough of us take action right now. Here's the deal: on Monday, the FCC's decision to repeal net neutrality rules will take effect. This would give telecommunications companies the ability to potentially slow a website like Netflix down in an attempt to force you to use their preferred sites. This kind of power could have grave consequence for our economy and our free speech rights, stifling innovative and activism fundamental to the internet as we know it. We have a plan to stop this from happening. All we need is one more Republican Senator to join us in supporting a resolution overturning the FCC 's decision. Help us turn up the heat and get that vote: ** The basic principle of net neutrality is simple: anyone should be able to access any website or web service they want equally and anyone can make their own site or service and make it accessible to anyone with internet access. It's a genius idea that enables entrepreneurship on a level playing field -- and that openness and sense of opportunity are particularly crucial for historically disadvantaged communities. On the internet, anyone can become an overnight sensation based on the quality of their work, regardless of their gender, the color of their skin, who they love, or where they were born. That's why we have to work hard to make our voices heard in this process -- especially since we're running out of time and we're so close to forcing a vote on overturning the FCC's awful decision to gut net neutrality. . ** Thanks for all you do, --Team Kamala If you wish to stop receiving our emails or change your subscription options, please Democrats.com, Box 1452, New York, NY 10276

name: Bill McKibben, The Sanders Institute
subject: 100% Renewable Energy is Within Our Reach
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
The world cannot afford to continue to rely on fossil fuels. Scientists now tell us that at current rates, within a decade we'll likely have put enough carbon in the atmosphere to warm the earth past the Paris climate targets. We are running out of time.
[**However, we also now know what we can do, and we're increasingly certain it can be done: We have to switch off coal, oil, and gas, and switch on 100% water, wind, and sun.**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7abdb/bed43eb/53a403a9/75fd8275/3662799991/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/04/18/2018/achieving-100-renewable-energy")
Cities and states around America are already moving towards this goal. Countries around the world are outpacing us: transport in the Netherlands is run increasingly on wind power, Santiago's train system will run entirely on solar power soon, China can power provinces the size of Texas for a week straight.
The technology to implement 100% renewables already exists and we have public opinion on our side. A majority of Americans favor government action to increase the development of renewable energy.
Corporate interests and politics stand in our way. I know we can overcome them. We must.
[**Learn more about this fight for 100% Renewables by reading my article.**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7abdb/bed43eb/53a403a9/75fd8275/3662799991/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/04/18/2018/achieving-100-renewable-energy")
Thanks for staying engaged,
Bill McKibben

name: Jeremy Ring
subject: Politics don't belong in public retirement systems
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND APPROVED BY JEREMY RING, DEMOCRAT, FOR CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER |
body:
Friends and Neighbors --

One of the most consequential responsibilities of the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) is their role as one of three Trustees to the Florida Retirement System (FRS).

Along side the Governor and Attorney General the Trustees are responsible for over oversight of over $160 billion in capital and the retirements of over 600,000 Floridians. Maximizing returns to the beneficiaries not politics is what is supposed to drive their decisions, in fact in 2010 the Securities and Exchange Commission implemented a rule that prohibits firms from receiving investment fees from public pension systems if their executives donate campaign cash to pension overseers -- like Florida's Trustees.

Federal law and the roles of the Trustees are pretty cut and dry, politics don't belong in public retirement systems. Despite all of this, a review of government documents by several news outlets have uncovered that a campaign committee led by Governor Scott raked in donations from two private equity executives after he and the other Trustees directed state pension investments to their firms.

Governor Scott’s actions are not only unethical and outright ILLEGAL — but they also compromise the integrity of the FRS.

name: Tom Perez
subject: We're suing the Trump campaign, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
As a DNC supporter, I want you to be the first to know: Today, the DNC is filing a civil lawsuit alleging that Russia perpetrated a brazen attack on American democracy during the 2016 election, and fo
---
Derek --

As a DNC supporter, I want you to be the first to know: Today, the DNC is filing a civil lawsuit alleging that Russia perpetrated a brazen attack on American democracy during the 2016 election, and found a willing partner in the Trump campaign.

Here's why: A major part of Russia's attack on American democracy was the cyberattack on the DNC and theft of the DNC's proprietary information. This stolen information was then released to the public by Russian agents and WikiLeaks to damage the Democratic Party and influence the 2016 election.

We're taking this action because we believe no one is above the law, and we must pursue every avenue of justice against those who engaged in this illegal activity against the DNC and our democracy.

This is a patriotic -- not partisan -- move. As Sen. John McCain said, "When you attack a country, it's an act of war. And so we have to make sure that there is a price to pay, so that we can perhaps persuade the Russians to stop these kind of attacks on our very fundamentals of democracy."

We believe that Russia found a willing partner in the Trump campaign, who shared their goal of damaging the Democratic Party and helping elect Donald Trump. The Trump campaign had repeated secretive communications with Russian agents and WikiLeaks. Russian agents told Trump advisors that the Kremlin wanted to help Trump and had stolen emails and other material that could damage Trump's opponent. And Trump's closest political advisor, Roger Stone, had advanced knowledge of a major release of stolen Democratic emails.

It is a disgrace to our country that instead of taking actions to protect American democracy, the Trump campaign put itself first. And if the occupant of the Oval Office refuses to protect our democracy, it's up to us.

Putting together a lawsuit like this, with all the proper documentation, has taken some time. That's why we're filing it today, both to seek justice and deter further attacks on our democracy.

Make no mistake about it, Derek: Our number one focus at the DNC is, and remains, electing Democrats from the school board to the Oval Office -- and we're winning all across this country.

We're going to keep our eyes on the prize: taking back the House and Senate, winning back state legislatures, and moving our country in the right direction.

But winning elections also means protecting elections, and today we're doing just that.

Thank you, as always, for your support.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

subject: My favorite part of the campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
We all want what's best for our state and our country. In 2018, that means re-electing Joe to the Senate. But I know that's going to take all of our hard work and determination. |
---

| | Peter,

When I ran for governor in 2016, meeting with Hoosier voters was my favorite part of the campaign. And talking with as many voters as we did was only possible because we invested early in a solid organizing team across the state.

Now, I'm back on the trail helping my friend, Joe Donnelly, who's running for re-election to the U.S. Senate. It's going to be a tough race, so it's critical that Joe's team is able to reach out to voters in each of Indiana's 92 counties. And that means they need infrastructure: Organizers, volunteers, and field offices.

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Why Mike Huckabee and Tom Cotton are attacking me on Twitter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
Here's how Wednesday went, Friends:
In the morning, Republican Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton took to Twitter to attack me. Then that afternoon, former presidential contender Mike Huckabee piled on, too!
Why? Because I had the nerve to stand up for women and our right to choose.
Friends, here's something Tom and Mike should know: **I won 't be intimidated - I'm going to keep fighting for international family planning and women's reproductive rights.**
I'll be up for reelection soon, and Republicans like Cotton and Huckabee are going to do everything they can to replace me with an anti-choice extremist who'd make it harder for women to get the health care they need. **So today, I need you to give $5 - or whatever you can spare - to fight against extremism and for women's rights. Here's the best link: [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jeanne-shaheen](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jeanne-shaheen?refcode=jtk845-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500)**
This is 2018. A woman's health care choices should be between her and her doctor. I'm proud to stand with you and for a woman's right to make her own reproductive health care decisions.
Thank you for this.
Jeanne

**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**We’ve got a lot going on in the months of April and May! **
****
**We’re gearing up for the 2018 election cycle here in New Hampshire – we’re raising money and recruiting candidates.**
****
[**Every Wednesday evening we are hosting open house at the NHGOP HQ for anyone interested in learning more about running for office.**](nh.gop/recruitment)
****
[**Monday, April 23rd, Cigars w/Senate President Morse, Speaker Chandler, and RNC Co-Chair Paduchik in Concord.**](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbWVJNyibvWwQ8oBRuSmEg4E-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MkYfLX-2Fitp52Vk07ITVH6OF0xwuGbFot7TOg8J-2FE10Cg1ojPZNcKhl9OKEI2HLI3puphrdIaGE5lTxQbNEikxrnQ6Js6YczSkxj36VZNpAxOF7oO55eenfSlOJnVtYcSEh1a7whpnK77QPG4G5A5wfQi8m-2BCdXZwGcKZx7HKAcw2clLhPot3ClwuDP6VzjkkynlUXwt4v8f16ebaPm0nzaxhcAxw5RDfcRAuDqVSczMkLn8x-2FtH14-2Bpt7cdJXNWb2wm282O0xegw-2F2hLTUfy-2FCQ-3D-3D)
****
**[Wednesday, April 25th, Senate PAC Fundraiser with Rudy Giuliani in Windham.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyJqxLw-2BEOOh7OJIuEYZuCRmCBONWMBN-2Fd1S43nut3HI4JjUYq1QkbHutDpdSoDfTW4m8TQXmrvO6h-2FucZdMY1YTAmTi-2BZJcmQxnA-2FgxEDYjsklXqvv1ok4FWML7yIOgGdg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MkYfLX-2Fitp52Vk07ITVH6OF0xwuGbFot7TOg8J-2FE10Cg1ojPZNcKhl9OKEI2HLI3puphrdIaGE5lTxQbNEikxrossp2sMfDG4up9U8QPspm-2B9y-2BmPHwtrqLsWAjnCOtIuqFSDIRemyh02RxnGWSBlSKmhWrITzk529VHtXQNoaDIBhfp0kPKK1p3p-2FuYNUk3nXBVCE-2B2GTNW-2Fs-2F0sY-2FP0n6rXMzaeTryDGc4bJWUlYFHYVEXcpx-2Bktqrx9EPk499qnByyhVQI8RyzZWGbM8k58w-3D-3D)**
****
[**Saturday, May 12th, NHRSC 2018 Convention in Meredith.**](nh.gop/convention)
****
**We are 200 days out from election day – every single day will count in our efforts to hold and grow our majorities. Not only here in New Hampshire, but in Washington. We need to make sure we take back both Congressional seats and send reinforcements to President Trump. **
****
**Here in New Hampshire and in Washington, Republicans are getting the job done and we need to continue moving in the right direction.**

**So please get involved today. Start talking with your friends and neighbors about getting involved. Remind them of all the good things that have happened under Republican leadership:[lower taxes, less regulation, constitutional carry. ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbaHNPRK8Me-2B1utWw5e1trdq8r15bJkI4BGirX9k1oEnc_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MkYfLX-2Fitp52Vk07ITVH6OF0xwuGbFot7TOg8J-2FE10Cg1ojPZNcKhl9OKEI2HLI3puphrdIaGE5lTxQbNEikxrni6IOpXi8IYBP4E9O-2F1w0OZxh-2Bj74GF5Im1jPDH-2FGCGw3YuDLkFFBxlykYfoo0Vb3I9kWzsVaSSIawg-2BconRhz03MaVSyj-2FgtYjhjSs26Fgi448-2BJO-2F2sQprXm3avKWlsA40qcXRZtKPo6-2BswyLpizhoCPJELnQWm-2BVjT4O-2BkkkhejUhzjydr8TQeDuXqu-2Bug-3D-3D)**
****
**Invite someone new to your Town, County, or City Committee meeting. Write a letter to the editor. Tweet, Facebook, email, and talk, talk, talk. We have much to be proud of and we need to let everyone know that New Hampshire is leading among New England economies because of Republican leadership.**
****

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

**The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party.**

| **_It's About Leadership_** When you serve as an elected official, you're expected to be a leader. Leadership is about working on big issues, and it's about letting people know where you stand. And today, we're seeing a void of leadership from Democrat Joe Donnelly.

As many out-of-touch Democrats are coming out and saying they plan to vote against CIA Director Mike Pompeo's confirmation as our nation's next secretary of state, Democrat Joe Donnelly is mum on the issue. He's refusing to answer the simple question on whether he'll support President Trump's nominee as our nation's top diplomat.

Pompeo is more than qualified for the post, with glowing reviews from national security experts across the country. He's a U.S. Army veteran who graduated top of his class at West Point -- a true leader. And when he was confirmed as CIA director, he had the support of 14 Senate Democrats, including Donnelly. What makes this vote any different?

Recent secretaries of state -- from Colin Powell to Hillary Clinton -- have received almost unanimous consent from the Senate. Again, what makes Pompeo any different?

Of course, it's no wonder that we're seeing this void of leadership from Democrat Donnelly. He's been critical of President Trump's foreign policy agenda, and when we need his vote the most, it almost never comes.

Hoosiers deserve a U.S. Senator who knows how to lead, and who will support President Trump's agenda in Washington. And time-after-time, we're learning that this senator can't be Democrat Donnelly.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Pictured: Chairman Kyle Hupfer joins Governor Eric Holcomb for a Q &A session at the Clark County Lincoln Day Dinner._

| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_Infrastructure: It's More Than Just Roads_**
You probably know that **Governor Eric Holcomb** 's major priorities are his five governing pillars -- one of which is _maintaining and building the state's infrastructure_. But you might not know that infrastructure means a whole lot more than just roads and bridges. It also includes the things that connect us all -- like the infrastructure that delivers water to our homes.

That's why Scott Pruitt, the administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, was in Indiana yesterday to meet with Governor Holcomb and local leaders to discuss lead cleanup efforts in Northwest Indiana. And while he was in town, Administrator Pruitt sat down with Governor Holcomb to [sign an agreement](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fd5cc59911&e=1acd9bb3d6) to accelerate this process in Indiana.

The state will receive an infusion of $436 million from the federal government to improve aging water infrastructure across the state. As Governor Holcomb said, this will give us, "a head start on rehabilitating contaminated areas in Whiting and Hammond."

Thanks, Governor Holcomb and Administrator Pruitt for your work this week -- and to the Trump administration for this important partnership!

Leadership is about working on big issues, and it's about letting people... | | | | | | | It's About Leadership When you serve as an elected official, you're expected to be a leader. Leadership is about working on big issues, and it's about letting people know where you stand. And today, we're seeing a void of leadership from Democrat Joe Donnelly. As many out-of-touch Democrats are coming out and saying they plan to vote against CIA Director Mike Pompeo's confirmation as our nation's next secretary of state, Democrat Joe Donnelly is mum on the issue. He's refusing to answer the simple question on whether he'll support President Trump's nominee as our nation's top diplomat. Pompeo is more than qualified for the post, with glowing reviews from national security experts across the country. He's a U.S. Army veteran who graduated top of his class at West Point -- a true leader. And when he was confirmed as CIA director, he had the support of 14 Senate Democrats, including Donnelly. What makes this vote any different? Recent secretaries of state -- from Colin Powell to Hillary Clinton -- have received almost unanimous consent from the Senate. Again, what makes Pompeo any different? Of course, it's no wonder that we're seeing this void of leadership from Democrat Donnelly. He's been critical of President Trump's foreign policy agenda, and when we need his vote the most, it almost never comes. Hoosiers deserve a U.S. Senator who knows how to lead, and who will support President Trump's agenda in Washington. And time-after-time, we're learning that this senator can't be Democrat Donnelly. -Kyle Hupfer Pictured: Chairman Kyle Hupfer joins Governor Eric Holcomb for a Q &A session at the Clark County Lincoln Day Dinner. | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Infrastructure: It's More Than Just Roads You probably know that Governor Eric Holcomb 's major priorities are his five governing pillars -- one of which is maintaining and building the state's infrastructure. But you might not know that infrastructure means a whole lot more than just roads and bridges. It also includes the things that connect us all -- like the infrastructure that delivers water to our homes. That's why Scott Pruitt, the administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, was in Indiana yesterday to meet with Governor Holcomb and local leaders to discuss lead cleanup efforts in Northwest Indiana. And while he was in town, Administrator Pruitt sat down with Governor Holcomb to to accelerate this process in Indiana. The state will receive an infusion of $436 million from the federal government to improve aging water infrastructure across the state. As Governor Holcomb said, this will give us, "a head start on rehabilitating contaminated areas in Whiting and Hammond."
